meta_description: Learn how subtractive manufacturing shapes parts by removing material. Explore CNC, drilling, laser cutting, and how it compares to additive methods.

sections.heading: What is subtractive manufacturing?
sections.text: What is subtractive manufacturing? Subtractive manufacturing is a time-tested method of creating parts by removing material from a solid block. It allows engineers to achieve tight tolerances, great surface finishes, and strong material performance.

sections.heading: How does subtractive manufacturing differ from additive?Â Â
sections.text: Subtractive manufacturing removes material from a solid blockâalso called a blank or workpieceâto form a final shape. As Michelangelo supposedly said of sculpting the David, "I just removed everything that was not David." Today, instead of chisels and marble, engineers use precision tools to shape metals and plastics with the same principle in mind.Â Some of the most well-known subtractive methods include CNC milling , CNC turning , drilling, grinding, and laser cutting. While each technique removes material in a different wayâmilling uses rotating tools, laser cutting uses concentrated beams of lightâthey all carefully subtract material to match a final design.Â This article covers...
